channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Judge who blocked release of Trump report was 'plainly' wrong, special counsel tells appeals court
item.description: The Justice Department has asked a federal appeals court to move swiftly in reversing a judge’s order that had blocked the agency from releasing any part of special counsel Jack Smith’s investigative report on Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: Eric Tucker, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Schools nationwide brace for Trump, including measures compelling teachers not to cooperate with ICE
item.description: K-12 school districts, universities and state-level education leaders in the U.S. are drawing up plans to protect students from Trump's immigration enforcement.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Panama Canal administrator pushes back against Trump's assertions of Chinese meddling
item.description: PANAMA CITY (AP) — The administrator of the Panama Canal said Friday that the vital waterway will remain in Panamanian hands and open to commerce from all countries, rejecting claims by President-elect Donald Trump that the United States should take it over.
item.dc:creator: ALMA SOLÍS, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: LA Times owner: It's 'crazy' we don't elect people with competence
item.description: Los Angeles Times owner Dr. Pat Soon-Shiong said it's "crazy" his state "doesn't elect leaders with competence.
item.dc:creator: Leland Vittert, NewsNation

item.title: Biden is still considering pardons for people who have been criticized or threatened by Trump
item.description: President Joe Biden says he is still considering whether to give pardons to people who have been criticized or threatened by President-elect Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: Zeke Miller And Colleen Long, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Supreme Court to weigh reinstating Obamacare care requirements struck down by lower court
item.description: The Supreme Court agreed Friday to consider some reinstating preventative care coverage requirements under the Affordable Care Act that were struck down by a lower court.
item.dc:creator: Lindsay Whitehurst, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Judge holds Rudy Giuliani in contempt of court for continued lies about Georgia election workers
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Rudy Giuliani was found in contempt of court Friday for the second time in a week, as a federal judge warned him he could be sent to jail if he doesn't stop spreading lies about two former Georgia election workers who won a $148 million defamation judgement against him. U.S.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL KUNZELMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: FBI must be independent and above the partisan fray, outgoing director says in farewell address
item.description: Outgoing FBI Director Christopher Wray says the bureau must remain independent, above the partisan fray and “committed to upholding the rule of law.
item.dc:creator: Eric Tucker, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: TikTok gets frosty reception at Supreme Court in fight to stave off ban
item.description: TikTok received a frosty reception in its fight to save the platform at the Supreme Court, which during oral arguments Friday expressed sympathy with the government’s national security concerns about the platform’s ties to China.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, NewsNation

item.title: Biden to deliver prime-time farewell to nation on Wednesday from Oval Office
item.description: President Joe Biden will deliver a farewell address to the nation Wednesday from the Oval Office, five days before President-elect Donald Trump is sworn in.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Montana AG asks Supreme Court to uphold law requiring parental consent for a minor's abortion
item.description: Montana AG Austin Knudsen petitioned the U.S. Supreme Court to uphold a parental consent law for minors' abortions that his state's high court struck down last year.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: DOGE reps launch meetings with federal staffers in effort to cut government waste: report
item.description: DOGE representatives are reportedly holding meetings with federal staffers in an effort to cut government spending waste.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Nevada's Cortez Masto to reintroduce bill to fund fight against wildfires
item.description: In the wake of the devastating California wildfires, Nevada Democratic Sen. Catherine Cortez Masto plans to reintroduce a proposal in Congress to better fund fire departments, interagency coordination and recovery efforts.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Biden extends time in US for 800,000 Venezuelans, Salvadorans as Trump readies immigration crackdown
item.description: MIAMI (AP) — About 600,000 Venezuelans and more than 230,000 Salvadorans already living in the United States can legally remain another 18 months, the Department of Homeland Security said Friday, barely a week before President-elect Donald Trump takes office with promises of hardline immigration pol
item.dc:creator: GISELA SALOMON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump vows to shed light on mystery drones when president
item.description: Trump, who will be inaugurated as the 47th president on Jan. 20, criticized the Biden administration's handling of the mystery drones.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Investigation clears Duluth mayor’s campaign manager and partner
item.description: An external investigation commissioned by the city of Duluth has found Mayor Roger Reinert’s partner and campaign manager Amber Gurske did not act improperly through her communications with city staff early in his administration.
item.dc:creator: Dan Kraker, MPR News

item.title: Supreme Court seems likely to uphold a federal law that could force TikTok to shut down on Jan. 19
item.description: The Supreme Court seems likely to uphold a law that would ban TikTok in the United States beginning Jan. 19 unless the popular social media program is sold by its China-based parent company.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Freedom Caucus members to huddle with Trump on strategy
item.description: Members of the House Freedom Caucus say they will make the case for their preferred policies and strategy when they meet with President-elect Trump at Mar-a-Lago on Friday to discuss his ambitious legislative agenda — bringing preferences that at times clash with those of Trump. Rep.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Appeals court denies bid to block public release of special counsel's report on Trump Jan. 6 probe
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A federal appeals court on Thursday denied a bid to block the public release of special counsel Jack Smith's report on President-elect Donald Trump's efforts to overturn his 2020 election loss. The 11th U.S.
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER and ALANNA DURKIN RICHER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Remembering President Jimmy Carter: Not your stereotypical politician
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) -- Jimmy Carter was not your stereotypical politician. He was a deeply religious man and more of a moral leader than a political one.
item.dc:creator: Marvin Scott, PIX 11

item.title: Supreme Court rejects Trump’s bid to delay sentencing in his New York hush money case
item.description: A sharply divided Supreme Court has rejected President-elect Donald Trump’s bid to delay sentencing in his New York hush money case.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Supreme Court refuses Trump’s request to block Friday’s criminal sentencing
item.description: The Supreme Court in a 5-4 decision on Thursday refused President-elect Trump’s request to halt his criminal sentencing, now just hours away, closing off Trump’s last remaining pathway to avoid receiving his punishment for his 34-count felony conviction in New York.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, NewsNation

item.title: The Memo: Trump-Newsom feud ramps up as Los Angeles fires rage on
item.description: The feud between President-elect Trump and California Gov. Gavin Newsom (D) is hitting new peaks of intensity as wildfires rage on in Los Angeles. Trump has called on Newsom to resign, while the governor has hit back at Trump for politicizing the disaster.
item.dc:creator: Niall Stanage, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: LA Mayor Bass stonewalls reporter for 90 seconds at airport
item.description: Video taken on an airport jetway shows Los Angeles Mayor Karen Bass repeatedly ignoring questions from a reporter about her response to the wildfires ripping through the area this week.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, Fox 5

item.title: Senate Democrats join Republicans in voting to advance bill to detain migrants accused of crimes
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Newly in the minority, Democrats voted with Republicans on Thursday to advance legislation that would require federal authorities to detain unauthorized immigrants who have been accused of certain crimes — signaling that they will try and find spots to work with President-elect Don
item.dc:creator: MARY CLARE JALONICK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pennsylvania GOP Rep. Meuser considering run for governor
item.description: Rep. Dan Meuser (R-Pa.) signaled he is seriously considering challenging Pennsylvania Gov. Josh Shapiro (D) for his post in 2026. “I’m considering it,” Meuser told The Hill on Thursday. “I mean if I get in it’s because I think I can win and that means beating the incumbent which is Josh Shapiro.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Michelle Obama 'sends her thoughts and prayers' while absent from Carter's funeral
item.description: Michelle Obama wasn't among the mourners attending former President Carter's funeral service in Washington, but she says she's thinking of and praying for "everyone who loved and learned" from the late commander in chief.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Nevada Democrats join Republicans in bill to detain migrants accused of certain crimes
item.description: All five members of Southern Nevada’s congressional delegation said they support a Republican proposal to detain immigrants in the country illegally who commit certain crimes.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump says Newsom should resign as Los Angeles wildfires rage
item.description: The president-elect's criticism of Newsom has sparked a political battle over the fires that have killed at least five people and are far from contained.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, NewsNation

item.title: National Day of Mourning: What's closed as Jimmy Carter is laid to rest
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – Some facilities and operations will be closed on Thursday in observance of the National Day of Mourning following former President Jimmy Carter's death. Here's what's closed: All flags will continue to be flown at half-staff for 30 days from Dec.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, PIX 11

item.title: What's next for Canada? 5 questions will help decide the future after Trudeau
item.description: "We're in a unique situation in Canadian politics," Shachi Kurl, president of the Angus Reid Institute, tells NPR. Voters need to replace a lame-duck leader — and cope with threats from Trump.
item.dc:creator: Bill Chappell, MPR News

item.title: Jimmy Carter remembered by colleagues, family and friends as a devoted public servant
item.description: President Biden delivered a eulogy to former President Jimmy Carter at the National Cathedral service, noting the 39th president's faith and dedication to service.
item.dc:creator: Elena Moore, MPR News

item.title: Ex-FBI informant who fabricated bribery story about Biden and his son Hunter gets 6 years in prison
item.description: A former FBI informant who fabricated a story about President Joe Biden and his son Hunter accepting bribes that became central to Republicans’ impeachment effort was sentenced Wednesday to six years in prison.
item.dc:creator: ALANNA DURKIN RICHER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News
